description: All countertop surfaces templated, fabricated, set, and inspected — phase closes when superintendent and client have signed off on fit, finish, and functionality prior to plumbing trim-out.

categories.name: Pre-Template Verification
items: Cabinet installation fully complete and signed off before template appointment is scheduled — no exceptions
All upper cabinets, fillers, and crown blocking installed and plumb; no cabinet movement expected after template
Sink, cooktop, and undermount appliance rough openings confirmed on-site against approved selections prior to templator arrival
Backsplash tile selection and thickness confirmed with templator so reveal and scribing allowances are correct
Any waterfall edges, mitered returns, or book-matched slabs flagged on shop drawing before template is taken
Client-selected slab(s) physically tagged and held at fabricator yard; superintendent or client has viewed and approved specific slab faces

categories.name: Fabrication Coordination
items: Signed template drawing returned from fabricator with all cutout locations, edge profiles, and seam placement marked — reviewed and approved by superintendent before fabrication begins
Seam locations reviewed with client on shop drawing; seams positioned away from high-visibility sightlines and high-stress areas where possible
Edge profile confirmed matches approved selection (e.g., 3 cm eased, mitered waterfall, ogee) on shop drawing sign-off
Sink cutout type confirmed (undermount radius, farmhouse apron reveal) and matches plumbing rough-in stub locations
Cooktop cutout dimensions verified against appliance spec sheet on file
Fabrication lead time confirmed; countertop install date locked in schedule to protect plumbing trim-out start date

categories.name: Pre-Install Site Readiness
items: All walls behind countertop runs painted finish coat before install — painter will not have clean access after stone is set
Substrate checked for level along full run; no more than 1/8-inch deviation over 10 feet — shim plan in place for any high or low points
Plumbing stubs confirmed at correct height and lateral position so they will clear the underside of stone and cabinet floor cutouts
Electrical outlets, USB ports, and pop-up receptacles in island or peninsula verified at correct height before stone arrives
Pathway from delivery point to kitchen clear of debris, temporary stairs rated for slab weight if applicable (3 cm quartz slabs can exceed 18 lbs/sq ft)

categories.name: Installation
items: Slabs delivered and staged without contact with other slabs or sharp edges; no chips on finished faces or edges at delivery
Countertop sections set dry first and checked for fit, overhang consistency (typically 1-1/2 inch standard, 12–15 inch bar overhang per plan), and seam alignment before adhesive is applied
Seams tight, flush, and filled with color-matched epoxy; seam height variation does not exceed 1/32 inch across joint
Sink undermounted with manufacturer-specified clips, silicone bead continuous around full perimeter, no gaps visible from below
Cooktop and any built-in appliance cutouts verified for correct clearance per appliance manufacturer specs before stone is permanently set
All exposed edges confirmed to match approved profile with no chipping, rough grinding marks, or uneven polish
Stone scribed tight to wall at all backsplash contacts; gap at wall does not exceed 1/8 inch before caulk (to be caulked, not grouted)

categories.name: Quality & Punch
items: All seams inspected under direct light for color/vein match, flush height, and epoxy color blend
Overhang measured at each end and midpoint of every run; no run varies more than 1/4 inch from specified overhang dimension
Stone surface free of haze, adhesive residue, epoxy smear, and tooling marks across 100% of exposed faces
All cutout edges (sink, cooktop, outlets) polished or eased to match specified edge profile — no raw sawn edges left exposed
Waterfall or mitered joints checked for continuous vein alignment and flush exterior face
Any natural stone (marble, quartzite) sealed per fabricator specification; sealer product and application date logged in project file

categories.name: Sign-Off & Turnover
items: Superintendent completes full countertop inspection and documents any punch items with photos in project management system before client walkthrough
Client walkthrough completed; client reviews all surfaces, seams, edges, and cutouts under both natural and artificial light
All punch items from client walkthrough assigned to fabricator with due date; no plumbing trim-out scheduled until stone punch is resolved or formally accepted
Care and maintenance instructions for countertop material (sealing schedule, approved cleaners, heat/impact warnings) provided to client in writing
Fabricator warranty documentation collected and filed in project closeout folder
Client sign-off on countertop phase obtained in writing before plumbing trim-out crew is released to set fixtures
